description: Build the database schema with Supabase SQL migrations, authentication system with multi-provider support, identity verification/KYC flow UI, and role-based access control with Row-Level Security policies.
steps: Create supabase/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql with all database tables: users_extended (profile data beyond auth.users), profiles (public profile info, display_name, avatar, bio, trust_score), sellers (business_name, description, verified, commission_rate, rating), products (title, description, price, currency, status, seller_id, category_id, stock, featured), product_media (product_id, url, type, order, alt_text), categories (name, slug, description, icon, parent_id for nesting), orders (buyer_id, seller_id, status, total, commission, escrow_status), transactions (order_id, type, amount, status, payment_method, stripe_id), wallets (user_id, balance, currency, frozen_amount), withdrawals (wallet_id, amount, status, method, processed_at), reviews (product_id, user_id, rating, comment, verified_purchase), favorites (user_id, product_id), notifications (user_id, type, title, body, read, action_url), messages (sender_id, receiver_id, content, read_at), verification_documents (user_id, type, file_url, status, reviewed_by, reviewed_at), fraud_monitoring (user_id, event_type, risk_score, details, resolved), moderation_logs (moderator_id, action, target_type, target_id, reason, notes), admin_actions (admin_id, action, details), coupons (code, discount_type, discount_value, min_order, max_uses, expires_at, active), analytics_events (user_id, event_type, metadata, created_at), reports (reporter_id, target_type, target_id, reason, status), ban_system (user_id, banned_by, reason, expires_at, permanent), device_sessions (user_id, device_fingerprint, ip_address, user_agent, last_seen). All tables use UUID primary keys, created_at/updated_at timestamps, and soft delete (deleted_at) where appropriate.

Create supabase/migrations/002_indexes.sql with performance indexes on frequently queried columns: products(category_id, seller_id, status), orders(buyer_id, seller_id, status), reviews(product_id), favorites(user_id), notifications(user_id, read), messages(sender_id, receiver_id), fraud_monitoring(user_id, risk_score)

Create supabase/migrations/003_rls_policies.sql with Row-Level Security policies: users can read own profile, update own profile; sellers can CRUD own products; buyers can read all published products; orders visible to buyer and seller involved; reviews readable by all, writable by verified purchasers; admin bypass policies for moderation; wallets only accessible by owner
Create supabase/migrations/004_functions.sql with database functions: calculate_seller_rating(), update_trust_score(), process_escrow_release(), increment_product_views(), check_fraud_score()
Create src/lib/supabase/database.types.ts with TypeScript types generated from the schema (manual type definitions matching the SQL schema)

Create src/features/auth/components/sign-in-form.tsx - elegant sign-in form with email/password fields, social auth buttons (Google, Facebook styled as luxury buttons), Phone OTP option, 'Forgot Password' link. Framer Motion entrance animation.

Create src/features/auth/components/sign-up-form.tsx - sign-up form with email, password, confirm password, terms acceptance checkbox. Social auth options. Progress indicator showing this is step 1 of verification.
Create src/features/auth/components/social-auth-buttons.tsx - reusable social auth button group (Google, Facebook, Phone) with brand colors and hover effects
Create src/features/auth/components/otp-verification.tsx - OTP input component (6 digits) with auto-focus, paste support, countdown timer for resend

Create src/features/auth/components/verification-flow.tsx - multi-step KYC verification wizard: Step 1 (Personal Info: full name, DOB, country, address), Step 2 (Identity Document: upload ID card or birth certificate with drag-drop zone), Step 3 (Selfie Verification: camera capture or upload), Step 4 (Review & Submit with 24hr wait messaging). Progress bar at top. Each step has elegant animations.
Create src/features/auth/components/verification-status.tsx - status display component showing PENDING_VERIFICATION, APPROVED, REJECTED states with appropriate messaging and next steps
Create src/features/auth/actions/auth-actions.ts - server actions for signIn, signUp, signOut, resetPassword, verifyOtp using Supabase Auth
Create src/features/auth/actions/verification-actions.ts - server actions for submitVerification, uploadDocument, getVerificationStatus
Create src/app/(auth)/sign-in/page.tsx - sign-in page with centered card layout, dark background, gold accents
Create src/app/(auth)/sign-up/page.tsx - sign-up page with the sign-up form
Create src/app/(auth)/verify/page.tsx - verification flow page with the multi-step wizard
Create src/app/(auth)/layout.tsx - auth layout with centered content, minimal header (logo only), no footer

Update src/middleware.ts to implement route protection: public routes (/, /sign-in, /sign-up), protected routes (/dashboard/*, /marketplace/checkout), verification-required routes. Redirect unauthed users to sign-in, unverified users to /verify.
Create src/constants/roles.ts with role hierarchy enum and permission mappings: GUEST, PENDING_USER, VERIFIED_BUYER, VERIFIED_SELLER, MODERATOR, SUPPORT_STAFF, ADMIN, SUPER_ADMIN
Create src/lib/auth/permissions.ts with permission checking utilities: hasRole(), canAccess(), isVerified(), isSeller(), isAdmin()
acceptance_criteria: pnpm build succeeds with all auth components and database types
SQL migration files are syntactically valid
Auth pages render with proper luxury styling
Verification flow has all 4 steps with proper navigation
Middleware correctly defines route protection rules
Role system has complete hierarchy with permission utilities
TypeScript types match the database schema
All forms have proper validation patterns
findings: Build and lint pass cleanly. Next.js 16.2.6 shows a deprecation warning about middleware convention preferring 'proxy' but it still works. The otp-verification component needed a refactor to avoid calling setState directly inside an effect (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect rule). The database.types.ts empty Enums type needed Record<string, never> instead of {} to satisfy @typescript-eslint/no-empty-object-type. Auth pages render at /sign-in, /sign-up, /verify using the (auth) route group layout.
